hard.next_step: 01/07/26: Caroline reached out to EY if there is any update 29/05/26: extra questions answered and sent back 08/05/2026: Orals and Demo done with EY. EY expect appointment of preferred vendor within a month 23/04/26: demo with EY on May 8th 30/03/2026: Coupa puled out of this one - we have a fair cahnce 13/03/2026: RfP deadline 16/3 - EY is involved with Zycus support 02/03/2026: EY will front - we have a call to discuss today 20/02/2026: demo for the Telco SA client ( C ell C) 24/2 16/2/26: Follow up EY & Zycus Agentic AI Powered Procurement Solutions Demo today as Cell C liked our solution 27/1/26: Short intro done for Cell C as part of multiple vendors presentation - lead through EY SA
hard.ais_why:
• Product scope is traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSource, iSupplier) plus ML iAnalyze ‚Äî some AI/ML in analytics but no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no Merlin Intake selected. ML iAnalyze is the sole AI SKU and it's a spend-analytics product (machine learning categorisation) rather than the generative-AI/agentic stack. AI is not the headline of the deal.
• Formal Evaluation stage with EY South Africa engaged as advisor (jakob.schleu@za.ey.com, EY India team in support) and a strong Cell C buying committee including s.devries, lkekae, zndima and rfourie. Three meetings scheduled Feb-Mar 2026 but all three returned 'not_recorded' state in Avoma, so we cannot verify any customer-spoken AI sentiment from transcripts. BD_Engagement is Engaged per SFDC.
• Low confidence: SF Description is null and all Avoma meetings unrecorded. The score rests on product mix (ML iAnalyze hints at analytics AI) plus EY's involvement as an advisory partner. Default DOWN at 5/6 boundary held given the complete lack of explicit AI evidence from transcripts. Would move UP only if a recorded discovery surfaces buyer-side AI demand or RFP issuance specifies AI capability.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is likely front-runner after Coupa withdrew on 30 Mar, but EY's preferred-vendor decision is ~4 weeks overdue, the buyer has been silent since 27 May, and the 31 Jul close cannot absorb a 6-module MSA/SOW/InfoSec cycle.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-02-13 (143 days old). In Formal Evaluation since 2026-03-13 (115 days). Close date 2026-07-31 is 25 days out and already slipped once (30 Jun to 31 Jul on 2026-05-26). EY expected preferred-vendor appointment within a month of the 8 May orals (~8 Jun); today is 2026-07-06, so the decision is ~28 days overdue. Last buyer touch 2026-05-27 (40 days ago). No contracting has started; a 6-module S2P selection realistically needs 60-90 days post-award for MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec and finance sign-off, so 31 Jul is not credible. Stalled on the buyer's decision, not yet cold (under 60 days): Slowing.
evidence: Engagement state: cooling. Last Salesforce activity 2026-05-27 (40 days ago); only July action is Caroline chasing EY for an update (Next_Step__c 01/07/26).
Preferred-vendor decision overdue ~28 days (EY expected appointment within a month of the 8 May orals, per Next_Step__c 08/05/2026).
Coupa withdrew 2026-03-30 ('Coupa pulled out of this one - we have a fair chance'), leaving Zycus positioned to win but exposed to no-decision risk.
No direct Cell C champion; EY South Africa is the sole access channel (Next_Step__c 02/03/2026, 13/03/2026).
Economic buyer Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is mapped in Contact Roles but shows no direct engagement or pricing approval.
Amount cut from $200k to $150k on 2026-03-13 (field history) and no quantified ROI or savings case is on record.
Close date slipped 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 2026-05-26 with no contract kickoff; 25 days is insufficient for a 6-module MSA/SOW/legal/InfoSec cycle.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, savings target or ROI is documented anywhere: the named value fields are blank, all three Feb-Mar Avoma meetings returned not_recorded, and the Next_Step__c trail never cites a number. The $150k six-module S2P scope (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing) is unanchored to any measurable procurement outcome (spend under management, contract leakage, cycle time). Cell C is a South African telco under evident cost pressure (amount already cut from $200k to $150k on 2026-03-13), so an unquantified case is exposed to finance deferral. The move is to build a CFO-grade savings model with EY and Shaheen De Vries before the preferred-vendor call.
sources: Avoma: 3 meetings not_recorded, no buyer transcript

economic_buyer.narrative: Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is the clear budget authority on this deal, named in the Salesforce Contact Roles with a title that spans both finance and procurement approval. That makes the MAPPED, not a gap. What is missing is engagement: there is no recorded direct touch with her, no pricing conversation she owned, and no evidence she has personally endorsed Zycus, because every interaction has run through EY. The move is to get her into a value/pricing conversation before the award so the finance approval does not stall.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Shaheen De Vries, Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement, sdevries@cellc.co.za

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is a formal EY-run RFP (2026-005 'Contract Lifecycle Management Solution', per the 2026-05-27 Clari email), so the criteria center on contract lifecycle management with a wider source-to-pay footprint. Cell C progressed Zycus through a multi-vendor intro (27 Jan), a demo (24 Feb), orals plus demo (8 May) and a written-response round on implementation timeframe (27 May), which shows criteria around solution fit, delivery timeline and commercial terms. The precise scoring rubric is not documented because no call transcript was captured, so the criteria are inferred from the RFP process rather than confirmed verbatim.
sources: Task 2026-05-27: RFP 2026-005 CONTRACT LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT SOLUTION, request for implementation-timeframe responses
Next_Step__c 08/05/2026 orals and demo; 13/03/2026 RfP deadline 16/3

decision_process.narrative: The process is a structured EY-fronted RFP and the Next_Step__c sequence reads as the process map: multi-vendor intro through EY SA (27 Jan) -> agentic-AI demo (16-24 Feb) -> RFP deadline 16 Mar with EY support -> orals and demo (8 May) -> written implementation-timeframe responses (27 May) -> preferred-vendor appointment EY expected within a month (~8 Jun, now overdue). EY is the advisor and gatekeeper controlling access and cadence, with the Cell C procurement committee (Zeyn Khan, Marius Potgieter, Zuko Ndima) and finance exec Shaheen De Vries in the approval chain. The single risk in the process is that the final selection step has stalled past its own deadline.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are documented: there is no MSA, SOW, legal-review or InfoSec timeline on record, and no post-selection paper plan from the relevant stakeholder matters because the RFP is a Contract Lifecycle Management solution with a full six-module S2P scope, so contracting will not be trivial. Zuko Ndima (Contracts Specialist, Procurement) is on the Contact Roles and is the natural counterpart for the paper phase. Visibility would come from asking EY to share the intended contracting and InfoSec timeline the moment a preferred vendor is named.
sources: No MSA/SOW/legal fields or tasks on record

identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by Cell C on record: the pain fields are blank and no buyer call was captured. The scope (contract lifecycle plus end-to-end S2P) and the committee composition (strategic sourcing for marketing and infrastructure, property and procurement, contracts, operational finance) imply typical telco procurement pain (contract volume, rogue spend, spend visibility, supplier risk), but none is confirmed. Next_Step__c 16/02/26 records only that 'Cell C liked our solution' after the agentic-AI demo. The move is a direct discovery session with Zeyn Khan to surface and quantify the pain.

champion.narrative: There is no internal Cell C champion. Every interaction runs through EY South Africa (Jakob Schleu, jakob.schleu@za.ey.com), which is fronting the deal (Next_Step__c 02/03/2026 'EY will front', 13/03/2026 'EY is involved with Zycus support'), but EY is a third-party advisor, not a Cell C insider who will coach the deal or drive urgency. Five Cell C contacts are mapped (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Marius Potgieter, Shaheen De Vries, Mpho Mabaso) but none shows advocacy, coaching or agenda-setting. Zeyn Khan (Senior Manager Strategic Sourcing) is the strongest champion candidate given the procurement remit; securing him is the priority move.

competition.narrative: Coupa was a named rival in the multi-vendor RFP but withdrew on 2026-03-30 ('Coupa pulled out of this one - we have a fair chance'), materially improving Zycus's position; it stays on record as declined, not retired. No other rival is named in any field, next step or task. With the field thinned, the real current threat is not a competitor but no-decision / defer: the preferred-vendor selection is ~4 weeks overdue, there is no compelling event or contract-expiration forcing function, and the buyer has been quiet since 27 May. We win by removing the inertia, quantifying the cost of delay and giving EY a dated business case to force the call.
sources: Next_Step__c 30/03/2026 'Coupa pulled out of this one'

deal_movement.summary: Deal reached the decision threshold in May (orals + written RFP responses) then stalled: the EY-promised preferred-vendor appointment is ~4 weeks overdue, the close date slipped 30 Jun to 31 Jul, and the only July activity is Caroline chasing EY. Coupa's 30 Mar exit is the key positive.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa was the named rival but withdrew on 2026-03-30 ('we have a fair chance'), so it drops to a dormant/declined threat and stays on record as history. No other competitor is named in any source. The single strongest CURRENT threat is no-decision / defer: the preferred-vendor call is ~28 days overdue with no compelling event and 40 days of buyer silence, so the deal is more likely to stall than be lost to a rival.

best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is positioned to win after Coupa's exit, but the deal is EY-single-threaded, unquantified and stalled on an overdue decision. The three highest-impact levers are breaking to a direct Cell C contact, attaching a savings case, and pre-clearing the contracting path.
flags: Break EY-only access: land a direct line to Zeyn Khan or Shaheen De Vries so a Cell C insider, not the advisor, carries urgency into the finance approval.
Kill the no-decision risk: attach a quantified savings/ROI case to the $150k so the overdue preferred-vendor call has a business reason to land now.
Pre-clear the contracting path: with a CLM RFP and a six-module S2P scope, align Zuko Ndima early on MSA/SOW so a win does not stall in paper.

champion_strength.summary: No internal Cell C champion. EY South Africa (Jakob Schleu) is fronting the deal as a third-party advisor and is the sole access channel; none of the five mapped Cell C contacts shows advocacy or coaching. Zeyn Khan is the strongest candidate to convert given his strategic-sourcing remit. This EY dependency is the deal's central vulnerability.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The 16 Feb 'Agentic AI Powered Procurement Solutions' demo landed ('Cell C liked our solution'), yet the selected scope is traditional S2P plus ML iAnalyze with no Merlin or agentic SKU. There is latent AI appetite the current scope under-serves; a Merlin/intake narrative to the committee could lift value.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 Moderate. ML iAnalyze is the only AI SKU (spend-analytics ML), no Merlin or agentic AI selected. The agentic-AI demo drew positive interest but scope stayed traditional; no recorded call to verify current AI sentiment.

items.detail: Economic buyer Shaheen De Vries mapped but not directly engaged; no pricing approval or endorsement on record.

Amount cut $200k to $150k on 2026-03-13 with no quantified ROI; telco cost pressure exposes the deal to finance deferral.
Decision ~28 days overdue and close date already slipped once; 31 Jul is not credible without acceleration.

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling: last buyer activity 2026-05-27 (40 days), the July action is a Zycus-side chase of EY, and the preferred-vendor decision is overdue. Not yet dark (under 60 days) but drifting toward no-decision.

scope_change.detail: Amount cut from $200k to $150k on 2026-03-13 (field history), a 25% value drop with modules unchanged, a defensive cost-trim consistent with a telco under margin pressure and a signal to expect a phased rather than big-bang implementation.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: After Coupa withdrew on 30 Mar, Zycus is likely the front-runner in EY's Cell C S2P/CLM evaluation, but the preferred-vendor decision EY promised by early June is ~4 weeks overdue with no direct Cell C champion and 40 days of buyer silence, so no-decision is now the real threat.

win_position.text: Zycus is likely the front-runner: Coupa withdrew on 30 Mar ('Coupa pulled out of this one - we have a fair chance') and Cell C liked the 16 Feb agentic-AI demo, all inside EY's RFP 2026-005 (Next_Step__c 30/03/2026, 16/02/26).
No internal Cell C champion exists: every touch runs through EY SA (Jakob Schleu), so Zycus has no insider driving urgency or coaching the finance approval (Next_Step__c 02/03/2026, 13/03/2026).
The preferred-vendor decision EY expected by ~8 Jun is ~4 weeks overdue and the last buyer touch was 27 May, so the deal is drifting toward no-decision rather than a win (Next_Step__c 08/05/2026).

deal_momentum.text: The evaluation reached the decision threshold: EY ran orals plus demo on 8 May and requested written implementation-timeframe responses on 27 May, which Zycus answered on 29 May (Next_Step__c 08/05/2026, 29/05/26).
Momentum has stalled since: the only July activity is Caroline chasing EY for an update with no buyer reply (Next_Step__c 01/07/26).
The close date already slipped from 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 26 May with no contracting started, and 25 days cannot absorb a 6-module MSA/SOW/InfoSec cycle (field history 2026-05-26).

customer_commitment.text: Cell C advanced Zycus from a multi-vendor intro on 27 Jan through demo, orals and an RFP written-response round, showing sustained evaluation intent (Next_Step__c 27/01/26 to 27/05/26).
No Cell C stakeholder has committed directly: economic buyer Shaheen De Vries (Exec: Operational Finance & Procurement) has no recorded engagement or pricing approval (OpportunityContactRole).
Engagement is single-threaded through EY, not the five named Cell C contacts, and the buyer has set no date or next meeting of their own (Next_Step__c, LastActivityDate 2026-05-27).

deal_risk.text: No-decision / defer is the primary threat: the selection is ~4 weeks overdue with no compelling event or contract-expiration lever, on a telco that already trimmed the deal from $200k to $150k (Next_Step__c 08/05/2026, field history 2026-03-13).
EY-dependency risk: EY is the sole access channel, so if its priorities shift Zycus loses visibility with no direct Cell C relationship to fall back on (Next_Step__c 02/03/2026).
No quantified business case leaves the $150k exposed to CFO deferral at the finance-approval gate (SF value fields blank).

champion_strength.evidence: No internal Cell C champion; EY third-party advisor is the only access channel (Next_Step__c 02/03/2026, 13/03/2026).

deal_momentum.evidence: Decision ~28 days overdue; last buyer touch 27 May; only July action is a rep-side chase (Next_Step__c 01/07/26).

deal_risk.evidence: No-decision risk, EY single-thread, unquantified case, scope trimmed $200k to $150k.

customer_preference.evidence: 'Cell C liked our solution' after the 16 Feb agentic-AI demo and Coupa withdrew 30 Mar, but all signal is relayed through EY with no direct buyer preference stated.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings target, or quantified procurement pain in any field, next step, or call; value fields blank.

critical_signals.text: Coupa withdrew on 30 Mar, so Zycus is positioned to win, but the fight is now against inertia rather than a rival.
The 8 May orals and demo landed well and EY expected a preferred-vendor pick within a month, but that decision is now about four weeks overdue.
The economic buyer Shaheen De Vries, Cell C's operational finance and procurement executive, is mapped but has never been engaged directly.
The deal was trimmed from $200k to $150k in March and still carries no quantified savings case, leaving the price hard to defend at finance sign-off.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $200k to $150k on 2026-03-13 (field history), a 25% value drop with modules unchanged, a defensive cost-trim consistent with a telco under margi (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by momentum 57 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $200k to $150k on 2026-03-13 (field history), a 25% value drop with modules unchanged, a….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 57 (moving forward): Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 57, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
